library_preparation_preflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency, Docker service, environment variable, or system property is required; the artifact brings its compile/runtime dependencies transitively and the javac failure is due to 2.2.1 API changes in existing tests, not missing setup.
library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: No environment or system property setup is required. The gitexe provider shells out to the default `git` executable, configurable via `${user.home}/.scm/git-settings.xml`, but meaningful tests can avoid external services by covering command-line builders and output consumers with temp directories. When updating the existing tests for 2.2.1, adapt to the new tag API: use `CommandParameters.SignOption` instead of boolean signing arguments, and call `createPushCommandLine` on a `GitTagCommand` instance rather than statically.
risks: Tests that execute real git commits/tags may depend on local `git` availability and repository/user configuration; prefer command construction, parsers, or isolated local temp repositories unless Native Image verification proves more is needed.
The upstream project has test-only helpers such as `maven-scm-provider-gittest`, but they are not needed to fix the observed compile failure or reach meaningful generated coverage.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8354 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.maven.scm:maven-scm-provider-gitexe:2.2.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015
